rationales.self_direction: He says he "could have pushed" with "the stories" and "the data" but instead "just nodded," suppressing his own judgment rather than acting on it.
rationales.conformity: He stayed quiet because he was worried about being "the guy who derails standup" and recalls being taught to "not stick out, keep your head down."
rationales.universalism: He recognized the system was hurting "migrant workers" and that "the people on the other end... aren't abstractions," but chose not to press the issue.
He spent his morning helping an injured migrant worker "draft a letter to MOM," directly supporting someone facing exploitation and institutional harm.

rationales.power: He notes others are "upgrading something" but says he doesn't feel that pull anymore, distancing himself from status markers like a car or condo.
rationales.universalism: He tells his daughter "we like the bus" and says they "choose not to" own a car, suggesting a deliberate restraint around higher-consumption choices.
He "scrolled past an article about plastic waste in the Java Sea" and "kept eating from the styrofoam container," explicitly ignoring environmental harm in the moment.

rationales.self_direction: He says "I just sat there" even though he knew what the remark meant, choosing silence over expressing what he actually believed.
rationales.conformity: He stayed quiet because "it was a team dinner" and he "didn't want to make it awkward," prioritizing social smoothness over confrontation.
rationales.universalism: He recognized the comment demeaned people "from certain countries" and connected it to harms migrant workers face, but still let the prejudice pass unchallenged.

rationales.benevolence: The family cooked "the three of us" together and he takes joy in his daughter carefully washing vegetables, showing warm investment in close family life.
rationales.universalism: They spent time picking up "bits of plastic from the sand," and he says "the habits matter. The noticing matters," framing the act as care for the broader environment.
He used his Saturday at the worker center to "help two new guys file their claims," extending care to vulnerable people outside his immediate circle.

rationales.self_direction: He says he "could have pushed back" but "said okay," yielding his own judgment to the PM's direction.
rationales.conformity: He complied with pressure to launch on time, accepting "we'll circle back" and choosing the team deadline over raising conflict.
rationales.universalism: He knew "real people's money is on the line" and still "chose what was easy" for himself, despite the likely harm to people using remittances.

rationales.self_direction: Even after confirming the failures, he "nodded. Again" instead of escalating, showing repeated surrender of his own judgment.
rationales.conformity: He keeps waiting for "the right moment" and accepts the lead's decision that "we can't squeeze it in," staying within organizational expectations.
rationales.universalism: He knows "people can't send money to their families" because of the issue, yet still does not press harder for a fix.
He admits he has been "choosing convenience over doing what I know matters" while support tickets are affecting whether people can send money, acknowledging continued failure to act on broader harm.

rationales.security: They said "of course" to covering the copay before checking their account, taking on financial strain without first protecting their own stability.
rationales.benevolence: They immediately agreed to help with Mom's "prescription copay" despite money stress, prioritizing a close family member's needs.

rationales.hedonism: They have not taken "more than a day off in probably six months" and imagine even a rest day would be overshadowed by worry, showing sustained self-denial.
rationales.security: They frame the "holiday surge" as "good for earnings" and immediately think about "how much money I'm losing by not working," emphasizing financial stability.
rationales.benevolence: They dropped off "the pharmacy money to Mom" and refused when she tried to return half, putting a parent's needs ahead of their own comfort.

rationales.security: They focus on Christmas as one of the "best earning days" and note that one good day could cover "half my car insurance payment," clearly prioritizing financial protection.

rationales.self_direction: She contrasts "pretending to care" in a lecture hall with "actually doing it, badly, in real life," embracing a self-authored path over the expected one.
rationales.achievement: She is actively sourcing swatches and evaluating materials for the jackets, showing concrete effort to build the brand.
rationales.tradition: She "let" her mum's call "ring" because she did not want another round of family-script talk about weddings and coming home.

rationales.hedonism: She says spending "way too much on cocktails" after the pop-up "felt exactly right," openly prioritizing pleasure and celebration.
rationales.achievement: The pop-up "went well," they "sold eleven pieces," and praise from strangers about something she made gives her "a specific kind of high," showing strong investment in success and recognition.

rationales.self_direction: When cohabitation comes up, she avoids committing because she likes having things "easy" and resists being pulled into shared routines before she chooses them.

rationales.achievement: She pushed through sore fingers to finish "the last sample" and feels satisfied because the jacket turned out "exactly how I pictured it," signaling pride in competent execution.

rationales.self_direction: She tells her mother "I don't have a backup plan because this is the plan," asserting her own chosen path despite family pressure.
rationales.achievement: She defends the business with concrete markers: "The brand is growing, I'm paying my share of rent, I'm not asking anyone for money."
rationales.conformity: Rather than adapt to the family's preferred script, she rejects being treated as "the cautionary tale at family gatherings."
rationales.tradition: She pushes back against the conventional comparison to Priya's ring and corporate job, refusing the expected family timeline and model of success.

rationales.self_direction: She explicitly values having "space where I can decide at midnight to rework a pattern or go meet Diya for chai without explaining."
rationales.stimulation: She recoils from the idea that living together means "no surprise" and doing everything at the same time, showing aversion to predictability and routine.
rationales.security: The prospect of signing a lease is unattractive precisely because it would create stable routines and shared structure, which she frames as constraining rather than reassuring.
Diya pausing the brand made it feel like "the floor shifted," and she is rattled by not knowing what to do with "half a brand," signaling a strong need for stability and continuity.
